sourceMovie.title: Big Momma's House 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Big Momma's House 2 for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. John Whitesell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 2 Guns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Momma's House 2 clicked for you, 2 Guns (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Big Momma's House 2?
faq.answer: Try 2 Guns, White Chicks, Now You See Me 2—each shares DNA with Big Momma's House 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Big Momma's House 2?
faq.answer: Big Momma's House 2 (2006) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Big Momma's House 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Big Momma's House Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
